project: Closer — post-call assistant SaaS for high-ticket closers (Gabriel Galindo)
overall_status: Live in production at https://closer.gabriel-galindo.workers.dev, scoped to a single account (On Screen Authority) as of 2026-07-16. Functionally MOCK-ONLY: zero API secrets are set, so no real generation has ever run. Core app, auth, layout, single-account scoping, and CI/CD are complete and verified. Every remaining high-value task is blocked on third-party credentials (LLM key, Fathom key, GHL marketplace app), not on engineering effort. CRITICAL: the only live prompt template is the hypnotherapy-written master prompt, which actively mis-scores OSA calls (TASK-021) — now the sole template, so it affects 100% of calls.

tasks.title: Capture source requirements and master prompt
tasks.description: Transcribe Gabriel's two workflow walkthrough videos, save his master post-call prompt verbatim, and establish the project docs (README, NEXT_STEPS) as durable context for future sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Master prompt saved verbatim to a file
Pain points from the videos recorded (slow generation, too much output, manual copy/paste, tone rewriting)
README + NEXT_STEPS exist and explain the project to a cold reader
testing_criteria: Docs readable standalone without conversation history
tasks.notes: Prompt saved with Gabriel's annotated complaints. Source: two Zoom walkthroughs, 2026-07-10.
test_results: 2026-07-11: Docs reviewed for standalone readability — pass

tasks.title: Design UI direction via interactive mockup
tasks.description: Establish the inbox metaphor, all-black blue/violet/pink visual language, tiered scorecards, TL;DR highlights, sent-tracking, filters, Insights view, and responsive behavior — validated with Gabriel before committing to real code.
acceptance_criteria: Three outputs displayed separately, no scrolling past one to reach another
One-click copy per output
Tone control with AI-suggested default
Design approved by Gabriel
testing_criteria: All interactions exercised in a real browser
Responsive at narrow widths without clipping
tasks.notes: Iterated: warm Mac Mail -> 1995 Hackers terminal (rejected, unreadable) -> all-black w/ blue/violet/pink. Gabriel on 2026-07-12: 'This is incredible.' Mockup superseded by the real app but drove every UI decision.
test_results: 2026-07-11: Browser-verified nav, tone switching, copy, responsive scroll — pass
2026-07-12: Reviewed live with Gabriel — approved

tasks.title: Decide architecture and deployment model
tasks.description: Pivot from local macOS app to SaaS. Choose Cloudflare Workers + D1 + GitHub, email/password auth, vanilla front-end. Establish the code-vs-data separation guarantee so deploys never destroy user data.
acceptance_criteria: SaaS decision recorded and supersedes local-app docs
Code/data separation documented (repo = code only, D1 = data, additive migrations only)
Stack decisions confirmed by Ivan
testing_criteria: Plan reviewed against Gabriel call transcript for dropped requirements
tasks.notes: Gabriel on call: 'let's just build online for now.' Menu-bar app spec parked as future native companion. Ivan confirmed: email+password auth, D1, vanilla FE.
test_results: 2026-07-12: Decisions confirmed via AskUserQuestion — all four answered

tasks.notes: 100k PBKDF2 iterations. Constant-time compare implemented in verifyPassword.

tasks.title: Research and surface real integration auth methods
tasks.description: Ivan asked to avoid API keys and use logins where possible. Verify each provider's actual auth model against current docs and reflect it honestly in the Integrations UI.

No console errors
tasks.notes: Findings: GoHighLevel = true OAuth login (v2 API is OAuth-only, static keys deprecated). Fathom = self-serve key (own-account uses X-Api-Key; their OAuth is only for publishing apps to others). Claude/OpenAI = API key, no login exists for API access. Also corrected a cost assumption from the call: $20/mo Pro/Plus does NOT include API usage.
test_results: 2026-07-15: Browser — badges render: Claude 'API key', Fathom 'Self-serve key', GHL 'Login (OAuth)' + Connect — pass

tasks.title: Obtain LLM API key from Gabriel
tasks.description: Get an Anthropic API key from Gabriel and paste it into the Integrations screen (see TASK-032), then configure a provider-side spend cap as a second line of defense against runaway cost.
acceptance_criteria: Anthropic API key saved via the Integrations UI
'Test connection' passes against the real Anthropic API

Integration status shows connected
testing_criteria: Test-connection button returns success
A real call processes with model != 'mock'
git log contains no key material
tasks.notes: RESCOPED 2026-07-16: the install mechanism changed. Was `wrangler secret put ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (terminal + wrangler + Cloudflare token — Ivan reported he couldn't follow it, and it was the practical blocker on the whole project). Now: paste it into the Integrations screen (TASK-032). STILL BLOCKED, but only on Gabriel handing over a key — no longer on tooling. COST NOTE (unchanged, from TASK-013): Gabriel's $20/mo Claude Pro does NOT cover API usage — the API bills separately per token, so this needs its own billing setup. Suggest starting with a low spend cap ($20-50/mo) to prove the workflow. Possible funders: (a) Gabriel creates an Anthropic console account + billing, (b) Ivan funds a key initially and bills back. UPDATE 2026-07-16: an Anthropic key IS now saved in production and the integration status reads 'connected' — which in this app means the auto-test passed against the real API. A Fathom key is also saved (note: 'connected' for Fathom only means 'a key is saved' — there is no Fathom test endpoint yet, so it is UNVERIFIED). NO LONGER BLOCKED. Left in_progress rather than completed because two acceptance criteria are unverifiable from here: (a) confirm 'Test' passes on demand, (b) the provider-side spend cap lives in Gabriel's Anthropic console and I cannot see it. Ivan/Gabriel to confirm both.

tasks.title: Validate real LLM generation end-to-end
tasks.description: The Anthropic/OpenAI call code in src/llm.js is written but has NEVER run against a real API. Validate that the model reliably returns the strict JSON contract, that all 7 outputs generate, and that tone/outcome inference is sane on a real transcript.
acceptance_criteria: A real transcript produces a valid debrief JSON (no parse errors)
All 7 outputs generate (3 sms tones, 3 email tones, 1 ghl_note)
suggestedTone and outcome are sensible for the transcript
GHL note stays under 10,000 chars
Failure path is graceful if the model returns malformed JSON
testing_criteria: Process a real call transcript against the live API
Assert JSON parses and all output rows exist with model != 'mock'
Deliberately test a malformed-JSON response path
Measure wall-clock time (Gabriel's #1 complaint is waiting)
tasks.notes: RISK: extractJson() does naive brace-slicing and JSON.parse with no retry — a chatty model response will throw and fail the whole process. Real-world validation is likely to surface prompt/parse fixes. Also verify the parallel tone generation actually delivers the speed win promised on the call.

tasks.title: Obtain Fathom API credential (On Screen Authority)
tasks.description: Gabriel generates an API key in the On Screen Authority Fathom account (Settings -> Integrations -> API Access).
acceptance_criteria: Fathom key saved via the Integrations UI
A real authenticated call to Fathom's API succeeds
Integration status reflects a verified connection, not merely 'a key is saved'
testing_criteria: wrangler secret list shows FATHOM_API_KEY_OSA
A test authenticated call to Fathom's API returns recordings
tasks.notes: RESCOPED 2026-07-16 by TASK-030: was two keys (OSA + hypnotherapy); now ONE key for On Screen Authority only. BLOCKED: needs Gabriel to generate it. Self-serve, ~2 clicks, no developer account needed (per TASK-013 research). Note the OSA Fathom is a workspace Gabriel logs into rather than owns — missing info: whether his access level permits generating an API key there, and whether the plan tier includes API access. UPDATE 2026-07-16: a Fathom key IS saved in production. NO LONGER BLOCKED on Gabriel. But it is UNVERIFIED — the UI showed 'connected' merely because saving sets that flag; there was no Fathom test. TASK-035 adds a real auth check and will verify this.

tasks.title: Fathom auto-ingest on cron — calls arrive and process while nobody is logged in (the 'Gmail' behaviour)
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: 'I would like it to be like gmail, receiving mail (and in this case processing the data) even when I am not actively logged in... Ivan or Gabriel can log in and see the processed calls.' Today the cron fires every 5 min and does nothing (pollFathom is a stub), so NOTHING arrives while logged out. Wire the cron to the Fathom client built in TASK-035: pull new calls since the cursor, import them, and generate — all server-side, no session.
acceptance_criteria: Cron imports new Fathom calls with no browser session open
Newly imported calls are auto-generated so they are Ready when someone logs in
First-run cursor is seeded at key-save time — the cron must NEVER implicitly backfill history
Auto-generation is bounded to genuinely new calls only (never a backfill), so cost stays proportional to real call volume
No duplicate calls across polls (dedupe on Fathom recording_id)
Every cron run logs what it did — imported, generated, skipped, failed (TASK-040)
A cron failure never wedges the cursor or loses calls
Both Ivan and Gabriel see identical processed state on login (already true — single shared account + server-side data)
testing_criteria: Simulate a poll against real Fathom API and confirm call rows created
Run poll twice; assert no duplicates
Assert calls route to the correct account
Test API-failure path
Simulate a first run with no prior cursor: assert it does NOT import the entire Fathom history
tasks.notes: Currently a stub: src/index.js:286 logs 'Fathom polling not yet implemented'. Cron trigger (*/5 * * * *) is already registered and firing harmlessly. Will likely need a new migration to track last-polled cursor / external recording id for dedupe. This is THE feature that eliminates Gabriel's copy/paste — highest user value of the remaining work. FIRST-RUN CURSOR TRAP (raised by Ivan 2026-07-16, 'there are calls from today'): a poller keyed on 'fetch since last_polled_at' has no cursor on first run. Defaulting to now = today's calls are silently lost. Defaulting to epoch = it backfills Gabriel's ENTIRE Fathom history and — because createCall() auto-processes — fires an LLM run per call, unattended. At ~55 calls/wk over a year that is ~2,800 calls and plausibly $300-400 in one cron tick. DECISION: seed the cursor to now at key-save time; the cron only ever picks up genuinely new calls. Historical import is a separate, explicit, user-driven flow (TASK-033). RESCOPED 2026-07-16 to Ivan's 'Gmail' requirement. NOTE the sequencing: auto-generation must NOT be switched on before TASK-015 — not one real LLM generation has ever succeeded, and turning on an unattended cron that fires 4 paid calls per arriving call, with an unproven generation path, would fail silently on every call and could burn money doing it. Prove one manual generation first. Now also depends on TASK-040 so cron activity is observable — an unattended job with no log is undebuggable by definition.

Missing info: which GHL scopes are needed, and whether Gabriel's GHL plan permits marketplace apps. Possible solutions: (a) register the marketplace app for true OAuth, (b) fall back to GHL Private Integration Tokens (static, simpler, but reintroduces a pasted token — contradicts Ivan's stated preference), (c) defer GHL entirely and keep copy/paste for CRM notes, which Gabriel already tolerates.

tasks.title: Implement GHL OAuth connect flow
tasks.description: Build the real OAuth 2.0 flow behind the existing stubbed Connect button: authorize redirect, callback handler, encrypted token storage, and automatic refresh (GHL access tokens expire every 24h).
acceptance_criteria: Connect button initiates GHL OAuth and returns successfully
Access + refresh tokens stored server-side, never exposed to the browser
Token auto-refreshes before expiry without user action
Integration status reflects real connection state
Per-account connection (OSA and hypnotherapy connect independently)
testing_criteria: Full OAuth round trip against real GHL
Force token expiry and confirm refresh
Confirm no token material reaches client-side JS
CSRF state parameter validated on callback
tasks.notes: Connect button currently shows a toast explaining it needs the marketplace app first. Requires a new migration for oauth token storage (access_token, refresh_token, expires_at per account). Security note recorded in UI: OAuth still stores a sensitive token server-side — its win is click-to-connect UX + auto-expiry, not eliminating stored credentials.

tasks.title: Generate pre-call briefs and callback notes
tasks.description: GAP FOUND 2026-07-16: the pre-call brief is a designed, shipped UI feature with schema support, but nothing ever populates it. processCall never writes precall_brief, and callback_note is only manually patchable. On real (non-seeded) calls the follow-up banner will render empty.
acceptance_criteria: processCall populates precall_brief for follow-up calls
callback_note populated when the transcript implies a callback
Follow-up banner renders meaningful content on a real generated call
Empty/absent brief degrades gracefully rather than showing a blank banner
testing_criteria: Process a real follow-up transcript; assert precall_brief is non-empty
Assert the banner displays it in the browser
Process a closed-won call; assert no misleading follow-up banner
tasks.notes: Discovered by auditing production rather than trusting the demo. The seed data made this look finished — Priya/Marcus had hand-written briefs in seed.sql, masking the missing generation path. This is exactly the class of silent gap the work list exists to catch. Pre-call briefs were called out in ROADMAP as the highest-leverage v2 idea ('flips the product from post-call paperwork to pre-call advantage').

tasks.title: Retune the OSA prompt template to fix scoring bias
tasks.description: The live master prompt was written for pain/trauma-driven hypnosis sales. Applied to On Screen Authority calls it systematically under-scores Gabriel, because that style would repel sophisticated B2B buyers. Rewrite the single remaining template for OSA dynamics.
acceptance_criteria: The OSA template reflects fast-moving sophisticated-buyer dynamics rather than pain amplification
Template remains versioned (edit creates a new version, history preserved)
Scoring on an OSA call is no longer penalized for absent pain-amplification
Gabriel agrees the resulting scores are fair
testing_criteria: Run the same OSA transcript against old and new templates; compare scorecards
Confirm the scorecard dimensions still map to the UI's expected 10 dimensions
Confirm Gabriel agrees the new scoring is fair
tasks.notes: RESCOPED + PRIORITY RAISED 2026-07-16 by TASK-030: was 'diverge two templates'; now 'retune the one remaining template'. Priority medium -> high, because with hypnotherapy gone the ONLY live template is the hypnotherapy-written one, so 100% of Gabriel's calls are now scored by a prompt built for the wrong sales motion. Gabriel on call: 'those people are fast decision-making... they smell that from a mile away.' Templates are editable in-app and versioned, so this is likely a data change, not code — Gabriel should drive the wording.

tasks.title: Real LLM analysis for weekly edit suggestions
tasks.description: The Sunday cron correctly batches edits and gates on >=10 per (account, tone), but the 'analysis' it writes is placeholder text, not an actual LLM-written diff analysis. Suggestions are therefore not yet useful.
acceptance_criteria: Sunday job sends batched edit diffs to the LLM for style analysis
Produces a concrete proposed template body, not just prose
Only fires at >=10 unfolded edits per (account, tone)
Accepting a suggestion creates a new template version; rejecting changes nothing
Accepted edits marked folded_into_version so they are not re-analyzed
testing_criteria: Seed 10+ edits; trigger the job; assert a suggestion with proposed_body appears
Accept -> new active template version created
Reject -> template unchanged
Assert edits are not double-counted next run
tasks.notes: Batching rule and approval gate (never auto-apply) already implemented per the call decision — the missing piece is the actual analysis. NOTE: folded_into_version is never set by current code, so edits would be re-analyzed on every run. Must fix as part of this task.

tasks.title: SCOPE CHANGE: remove Hypnotherapy, scope to On Screen Authority only
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: 'we don't need any of the hypnotherapy components anymore. We just need the components for onscreen authority.' Remove the Hypnotherapy account, its integration slots, its prompt template, and all hypnotherapy references from seeds, config, and docs.

Its integration rows and prompt template removed
production_seed.sql seeds only On Screen Authority
Dev seed contains no hypnotherapy calls

Integrations UI shows only On Screen Authority
The accounts ARCHITECTURE is retained (single account, not ripped out)

tasks.notes: DECISION FLAGGED: removing the Hypnotherapy *instance and its data*, but RETAINING the multi-account *architecture* (accounts table, per-account scoping). Rationale: the abstraction is load-bearing (every query is account-scoped), it costs nothing to run with one row, and it is the foundation for TASK-027 productization which Gabriel explicitly wants. Ripping it out would be a large destructive refactor that buys nothing and blocks the roadmap. If Ivan wants the abstraction gone entirely, that is a separate decision. KNOCK-ON EFFECTS: TASK-016 now needs 1 Fathom key not 2; TASK-018/019 now 1 GHL sub-account; TASK-021 changes meaning entirely (no longer 'diverge two templates' — now 'retune the single template for OSA', and it becomes MORE important since the live template is hypnotherapy-written and actively mis-scores OSA calls).

tasks.title: Prompt caching: cache the transcript as a shared prefix across the 4 per-call requests
tasks.description: Ivan asked (2026-07-16) whether prompt caching would lower API costs. VERIFIED: yes, but it requires restructuring, not just adding cache_control. Today generateOutputs() sends the transcript FOUR times per call (1 debrief + 3 tone variants) and sets no cache_control anywhere. Caching is a strict prefix match and the transcript currently sits at the END of every prompt behind differing instructions, so the 4 requests share no cacheable prefix at all. Fix: invert to transcript-first as content blocks, with cache_control on the transcript block, so the awaited debrief writes the cache and the 3 parallel tone calls read it.
acceptance_criteria: Transcript is the first content block of all 4 requests and is byte-identical across them
cache_control {type: ephemeral} sits on the transcript block (5-min TTL default, NOT 1h)
usage.cache_creation_input_tokens is non-zero on the debrief request
usage.cache_read_input_tokens is non-zero on all 3 tone requests
Measured input tokens per call drop materially vs the pre-change baseline
Debrief still completes BEFORE the tone calls fire (the awaited ordering is what makes the cache readable)
Master prompt stays OUT of the cached prefix (it is only used by the debrief; caching it into the tone calls costs more and pollutes their context)
testing_criteria: Process a real transcript; assert cache_creation_input_tokens > 0 on request 1 and cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on requests 2-4
Compare measured total input tokens per call before vs after the change (must go DOWN, not up)
Test a short transcript (< ~1024 tokens): confirm it silently does not cache and the run still succeeds
Confirm output quality is unchanged — reordering transcript-before-instructions must not degrade the debrief or the drafts
Measure wall-clock time before/after (cache reads should cut prefill latency — Gabriel's #1 complaint is waiting)
tasks.notes: VERIFIED against current Anthropic docs 2026-07-16. Mechanics: cache write = 1.25x base input (5-min TTL), read = 0.1x; Sonnet 5 minimum cacheable prefix = 1024 tokens (a typical 29-41 min transcript is ~5-8k tokens, so it clears easily). Use the 5-min TTL — the debrief-to-tone gap is seconds, and 1h TTL doubles the write cost for zero benefit. ESTIMATED SAVING (no real measurements exist yet — the app has never made a real API call): ~58% off input tokens, which at Gabriel's ~55 calls/week is roughly $8/mo now (Sonnet 5 intro pricing) or ~$12/mo after the intro rate ends 2026-08-31. Total bill is only ~$28-42/mo either way, so this is a real but SMALL absolute win — priority medium, not high. The latency win may matter more than the cost win. TRAP 1 (why this depends on TASK-015): a broken prefix and an under-length prompt both fail SILENTLY with no error. The ONLY proof it works is non-zero usage.cache_read_input_tokens, which needs a real API key. Do not mark complete on 'the code looks right'. TRAP 2: if caching is wired up wrong, it costs MORE than today — every request pays the 1.25x write premium and nothing ever reads. TRAP 3 (load-bearing ordering): this only works because the debrief is awaited before Promise.all fires the tone calls. A cache entry is only readable after the first response begins. Anyone who later 'optimizes' by firing all 4 in parallel will turn 3 cache reads into 3 cache writes and silently raise the bill. Comment this in the code. BIGGER LEVER, NOT THIS TASK: output tokens are ~half the bill and caching cannot touch them. The 9-section debrief dominates output. Trimming it would cut cost AND address Gabriel's own complaint ('it's just so much, and I don't always read all of it') — that is the deferred v2 decision in NEXT_STEPS decision #3, not this task. REAL DATA 2026-07-16 (supersedes the estimates above): the first real Fathom call is a 62-minute call with a 75,655-char transcript ~= 19,000 tokens — roughly 2.7x the 7,000-token figure used in the original estimate. Recomputed: current input ~78k tokens/call (19k x 4); with transcript-prefix caching ~31k/call, i.e. ~60% reduction and roughly $20-25/mo saved at ~238 calls/mo (intro pricing), not the ~$8 originally estimated. Caching is now materially more worthwhile than first assessed, and the latency win is larger too. Re-baseline again once a few more real calls land — 62 min may be long-tail, not typical.

tasks.title: Paste-an-API-key form in Integrations (replace wrangler secret put)
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: 'I want an easy place where I can paste the API key... I don't clearly understand the method to install the API key.' Today the only way to install a key is `wrangler secret put` from a terminal with the Cloudflare token — which is the practical blocker on the entire project. Replace it with a paste-and-save field per integration in the Integrations screen, backed by D1, with a Test-connection check so a bad paste is caught immediately instead of surfacing as a failed call later.
acceptance_criteria: Each integration has a paste field, Save, Test connection, and Remove
Key is stored server-side in D1 and used by generateOutputs()
Key is NEVER returned to the browser — GET returns only a masked preview (e.g. sk-ant-...3f2a) plus last-updated
Test connection makes a real auth check against the provider and reports pass/fail
Saving a key flips the integration status to connected; Remove reverts it to disconnected
Existing `wrangler secret put` env vars still work as a fallback when no DB key is set (does not break the current deploy)
New migration is additive-only (ADD COLUMN), never drops or rewrites existing data
No key material is ever written to git or to logs
testing_criteria: Save a key via the UI; assert it persists to D1 and generateOutputs() picks it up over the env fallback
GET /api/integrations returns a masked preview and NOT the raw key (assert the raw value is absent from the response body)
Test connection with a deliberately bad key returns a clear failure, not a crash
Remove clears the value and reverts status to disconnected
With no DB key and no env var, generation still falls back to mock mode rather than erroring
Fresh migrate on an existing DB preserves all existing rows
tasks.notes: DECISION FLAGGED (2026-07-16): Ivan said 'this is a private site with access for only myself and Gabriel, I don't need the API key to be hidden.' Honoring the intent (make it easy, no terminal) while keeping the key WRITE-ONLY — stored in D1, never echoed back to the browser, masked preview only. Rationale: costs zero convenience, and it means a compromised shared login lets someone burn tokens through the app rather than exfiltrate the key and spend it elsewhere at unlimited scale. This is exactly the '$100k leaked key' scenario Ivan himself raised with Gabriel on the 2026-07-12 call. If Ivan wants the raw key displayed, that's a one-line change and his call to make. STORAGE TRADEOFF: the key sits in D1 in plaintext (not encrypted). Encrypting it would need an encryption key in a Cloudflare secret — reintroducing the exact bootstrapping problem this task removes. Accepted given the threat model, BUT note the real consequence: anyone holding the Cloudflare API token can read D1 directly. That token was pasted in plaintext chat on 2026-07-15 and Ivan declined to rotate it — so the API key stored here inherits that exposure. Worth revisiting if the token is ever shared further. Test-connection uses GET /v1/models on both Anthropic and OpenAI — a real auth check that costs zero tokens. Fathom's test is not implemented (their API shape isn't verified yet — don't guess); Fathom saves without a test. UNBLOCKS TASK-014, which gates TASK-015 and nearly all remaining high-value work. DONE 2026-07-16. Also corrected now-false UI copy: the Integrations subtitle claimed 'Cloudflare secrets / encrypted tokens' — keys now live in D1 unencrypted, so the copy was rewritten to say what is actually true (stored server-side, never sent back to the browser).

tasks.title: Explicit import of past Fathom calls (backfill)
tasks.description: GAP FOUND 2026-07-16 by Ivan: 'I don't think we ever added a way to pull in calls from the past.' Correct — nothing imports history. TASK-017's poller only fetches calls going forward from a cursor, so on day one everything already in Fathom (including calls from earlier today) is invisible. Add a deliberate, bounded import: pick a date range, preview what would be imported, choose which ones, land them in the inbox.
acceptance_criteria: An 'Import past calls' action lets the user pick a date range (e.g. today / last 7 days / custom)
Shows a PREVIEW list of matching Fathom calls with a count before importing anything
User selects which calls to import — never all-or-nothing by default
Imported calls land UNPROCESSED (status New) — import must not trigger LLM generation
occurred_at is set from Fathom's real call timestamp, not the import time
Re-importing an overlapping range creates no duplicates
Import is idempotent and safe to re-run
testing_criteria: Import a range; assert calls appear as unprocessed with correct original timestamps
Assert ZERO LLM calls fire during import (this is the money-bomb guard)
Re-import the same range; assert no duplicates
Import a range with 50+ calls; assert the UI stays usable and nothing auto-processes
Assert imported calls do not move the cron cursor backwards
tasks.notes: CRITICAL DESIGN CONSTRAINT: import != process. createCall() currently calls processCall() immediately, so reusing that path for a backfill would fire one LLM run per imported call. A 200-call import would be a several-hundred-dollar unattended surprise. Backfill MUST land calls unprocessed and let Gabriel choose which to spend tokens on — which also matches how he actually works (he only debriefs calls that matter). Depends on the Fathom API supporting a date-ranged list endpoint — UNVERIFIED, do not assume. Confirm the real API shape before building (same caution as TASK-017).

tasks.title: Manual call entry: capture the real call date, not the paste time
tasks.description: createCall() hardcodes occurred_at = datetime('now'), so a pasted transcript is stamped with the moment it was pasted rather than when the call happened. Pasting several of today's calls in one sitting stamps them all within minutes of each other, so the inbox orders them by paste order rather than call order; pasting an older call dates it wrongly outright. The compose screen has no date field.
acceptance_criteria: Compose screen has an optional date/time field defaulting to now
occurred_at persists the supplied value
Call list orders by real call time, not paste time
Omitting the field still works (defaults to now)
testing_criteria: Paste a call with an explicit past timestamp; assert the list shows that time and sorts correctly
Paste with the field left alone; assert it defaults to now
tasks.notes: Surfaced by Ivan's 2026-07-16 question about 'calls from today'. Small and UNBLOCKED — does not need any API key. Matters most right now because manual paste is currently the ONLY way to get calls in, so every call Gabriel enters tonight is mis-timestamped.

tasks.title: Fathom: real connection test + pull the single latest call
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: the Fathom card says 'No connection test available for fathom yet — the key is saved', which proves nothing; and he wants to pull just the most recent call rather than backfill history. Both are solved by one small Fathom integration: a real auth check, plus a bounded 'Pull latest call' that imports exactly one meeting as an unprocessed call.
acceptance_criteria: Fathom Test performs a real authenticated request and reports genuine pass/fail (no more 'no test available')
'Pull latest call' imports exactly ONE call — the most recent — never a history dump
Request is bounded by created_after so it can never pull the full history even accidentally
Result is sorted newest-first CLIENT-SIDE (Fathom does not document sort order — must not be trusted)
Imported call lands UNPROCESSED (New) — import must never trigger LLM generation
occurred_at comes from Fathom's real recording_start_time, not the import time
Client name derived from the external calendar invitee, not from Gabriel (the internal recorder)
Re-pulling the same call does not duplicate it (dedupe on Fathom recording_id)
Clear message when there are no calls in the window
testing_criteria: Bad key -> clean auth failure, no crash
No key -> clear 'save a key first' message, no crash
Verify the live Fathom response shape matches the documented schema before trusting it
Pull twice -> assert the second is reported as already-imported, with no duplicate row
Assert ZERO LLM calls fire during import (money-bomb guard)
Assert occurred_at matches the Fathom recording_start_time, not now()
tasks.notes: API VERIFIED against developers.fathom.ai 2026-07-16 (not guessed): GET https://api.fathom.ai/external/v1/meetings, header X-Api-Key, query params created_after/created_before/cursor/include_transcript, response {limit,next_cursor,items:[{recording_id,title,meeting_title,created_at,scheduled_start_time,recording_start_time,recording_end_time,transcript:[{speaker:{display_name},text,timestamp}],calendar_invitees:[{name,email,is_external}],recorded_by}]}. Rate limit 60 req/min. RISK: sort order is NOT documented — items[0] may be the OLDEST. Sorting client-side by recording_start_time desc is load-bearing, not a nicety. DESIGN: bound with created_after (default 7 days) so the request is inherently incapable of a full-history pull — this is the deliberate answer to the TASK-017 cursor trap. Lands unprocessed per the TASK-033 import!=process rule. Deliberately a narrow slice of TASK-033 (which remains the general date-ranged, multi-select importer). STATUS 2026-07-16: shipped and left in_progress deliberately. Everything testable without Ivan's real Fathom key is verified (endpoint+auth via a real 401, sort defence, dedupe, name derivation, transcript flattening, shape guard). The one unproven criterion is the live success-path response shape — chose NOT to read Ivan's stored Fathom key out of D1 to self-verify; a shape mismatch now yields a precise diagnostic rather than a wrong answer, so the cost of finding out via one button click is low. CLOSED 2026-07-16: success path confirmed against the live Fathom API by real usage.

tasks.title: Generation must survive navigating away + persist processing/failed state
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: 'took a long time to generate. I don't know if I clicked out too soon and it stopped the generation.' CONFIRMED — processCall() awaits all 4 LLM calls inline in the HTTP request and ctx is never passed to it, so a client disconnect can cancel the work. processed_at is only written at the very end, so a killed run leaves the call indistinguishable from 'New' with no error recorded. There is also no guard against starting a second run while one is in flight (double spend). Fix: persist a processing state machine, run generation via ctx.waitUntil so it completes regardless of the client, return immediately, and let the UI poll.
acceptance_criteria: calls carry an explicit processing_status: new | processing | processed | failed
Generation runs under ctx.waitUntil and completes even if the client navigates away or closes the tab
POST /process returns immediately (202) rather than blocking for the full generation
Starting generation on a call already processing is rejected — no double spend
A failure records processing_status='failed' plus the error, instead of silently reverting to 'New'
A stale 'processing' row (crashed worker) can recover rather than being wedged forever
Existing processed calls are backfilled to 'processed' by the migration
testing_criteria: Start generation, immediately abort the HTTP request client-side; assert the call still reaches 'processed'
Fire two concurrent process requests; assert exactly one generation runs (count LLM attempts)
Force a failure (bad key); assert status='failed' and the error is recorded and shown
Assert migration backfills existing processed calls correctly
Assert a call stuck in 'processing' past the stale window can be retried
tasks.notes: ROOT CAUSE of Ivan's report AND the prerequisite for the TASK-037 status indicator — you cannot show 'processing' in the inbox if the state is never written down. Cloudflare cancels request-scoped work on client disconnect unless it is handed to ctx.waitUntil; ctx is currently available in fetch() but never threaded through route() to processCall.
test_results: 2026-07-16: migration 0004 applies; backfill UPDATE proven against existing rows (processed_at set -> 'processed', else 'new') — pass

2026-07-16: POST /process now returns {ok,status:'processing'} IMMEDIATELY instead of blocking for the full generation; work completes in background via ctx.waitUntil (verified: 7 outputs + status='processed' ~3s after the 202) — pass
2026-07-16: DOUBLE-SPEND GUARD — process on an in-flight call returns already:true 'Already generating' and starts no second run — pass
2026-07-16: STALE RECOVERY — same call with processing_started_at 20 min old is allowed to restart — pass
2026-07-16: FAILURE PATH — bad key yields processing_status='failed' with the error recorded ('Anthropic 401: ...'), instead of silently reverting to a New-looking call — pass
2026-07-16: PRODUCTION migration 0004 applied, row counts identical before/after (users=1, calls=1, integrations=4) — no data loss — pass

LIMITATION: client-disconnect survival could NOT be faithfully tested locally — miniflare tears down the D1 binding the instant the client aborts (D1_ERROR internal error), so nothing survives in the emulator. The waitUntil mechanism itself is verified working; disconnect-survival rests on Cloudflare's documented waitUntil semantics and needs confirmation from real use.

tasks.title: Traffic-light status indicator in the inbox
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: wants an at-a-glance indicator per inbox row for new-unprocessed / processing / processed, using the existing palette with traffic-light-style rules.
acceptance_criteria: Every inbox row shows a status dot
Rules: hollow grey = New (not started), violet pulsing = Processing, blue = Ready, pink = Failed
Processing animates so it reads as live, and respects prefers-reduced-motion
State updates without a manual refresh while a call is generating
Colours reuse the existing blue/violet/pink language (pink already means 'bad' on the scorecard)
testing_criteria: Verify all four states render distinctly in-browser
Verify the dot flips new -> processing -> processed live
Verify reduced-motion disables the pulse
tasks.notes: Four states, three brand colours: grey stays neutral for 'nothing has happened yet' so pink can keep meaning 'something is wrong'.
test_results: 2026-07-16: all four states render distinctly in-browser — hollow grey (Sarah/new), blue (Jeffrey/processed), violet pulsing (Priya/processing), pink (Marcus/failed) — pass

tasks.title: max_tokens is too low for the 9-section debrief
tasks.description: llm.js caps output at 8000 tokens. The master prompt demands 9 sections including a GHL note of up to 10,000 characters (~2.5k tokens alone). A verbose run truncates mid-JSON, extractJson() throws, and the whole generation fails after paying for every input token — plausibly a contributor to Ivan's slow/failed run.
acceptance_criteria: max_tokens raised to a level that comfortably fits the full debrief
Truncation (stop_reason max_tokens) is detected and reported rather than surfacing as a JSON parse error
testing_criteria: Process a long real transcript; assert the debrief JSON parses and no section is cut off
Assert a truncated response yields a clear 'response was cut off' message, not 'No JSON in model response'
tasks.notes: Anthropic guidance: ~16000 is the safe non-streaming default; above that needs streaming to dodge HTTP timeouts. Quick, high-value, and likely to bite on the very first real run. CONFIRMED NECESSARY: the first real call is a 62-min transcript (~19k tokens). An 8000-token output cap against a 9-section debrief plus a <10k-char GHL note would very plausibly have truncated and failed after paying for every input token.
test_results: 2026-07-16: max_tokens raised 8000 -> 16000 on both providers — pass

2026-07-16: truncation now detected via stop_reason=='max_tokens' / finish_reason=='length' and surfaced as 'response was cut off', instead of failing downstream as the misleading 'No JSON in model response' — pass
2026-07-16: also added an explicit refusal check and a guard for a response with no text block

tasks.title: Activity log — durable record of failures, completions, usage and token spend
tasks.description: Ivan 2026-07-16: 'add a log system: logs failures, completions, all work... a document you can check as you continue to develop this which reports back information useful in finding bugs, fixing them, verifying components are working, and adding new features based on what gets used and what doesn't.' Add an events table written by every meaningful action, a filterable API, and an Activity view.
acceptance_criteria: events table records: generation started/succeeded/failed, Fathom pulls, integration tests, key changes, copies, sends, edits, logins
Failures record the actual error text, not just a status
Generation events record wall-clock duration
Generation events record real token usage (input/output/cache) and therefore actual cost
Key VALUES are never logged — only the fact a key changed
GET /api/events supports filtering by level and kind so a developer can answer 'what broke' quickly
An Activity view in the UI shows recent events with failures visually distinct
Logging can never break the app — a logging failure is swallowed, never thrown
testing_criteria: Force a generation failure; assert an error event with the real message
Run a successful generation; assert a succeeded event with duration and token counts
Assert no key material appears anywhere in the events table
Assert a deliberately broken logEvent does not break the surrounding operation
Verify the Activity view renders and filters
tasks.notes: Directly serves three needs at once: (1) Ivan's ask for a debugging/verification record, (2) the 'what gets used' question — logging copies/sends per output kind will show whether the 9-section debrief is actually read or whether only the SMS is, which is the evidence needed for the deferred 'trim the debrief' v2 decision, and (3) TOKEN/COST VISIBILITY, which the project currently has zero of — this is also what will empirically settle the TASK-031 caching estimates instead of guessing. Prerequisite for TASK-017: an unattended cron with no log is undebuggable. DONE 2026-07-16. llm.js now returns real summed token usage across all 4 calls, so cost is measured rather than estimated — this is what will empirically settle TASK-031's caching numbers and give the project its first real spend visibility.
